Empires & Puzzles Costumed Yunan Review: Lifeguard to the Rescue?

Yunan traded his royal armor for a lifeguard floatie, and honestly? It was a smart move. With a team-wide cleanse and a mana boost that would make a caffeine addict jealous, is this beach body ready for the modern battlefield, or is he just treading water?
Costumed Yunan: Baywatch on a Budget

When Yunan got his costume, he didn't just change his outfit; he changed his entire philosophy. Trading his "King of Sand" persona for the "King of Lifeguards," this 5-star Nature hero shifted from a selfish attrition tank to a supportive powerhouse. Released in 2021, the costume attempts to fix the glaring issues of the base hero by adding modern utility like a full-team cleanse and mana acceleration. This review focuses on the
, asking the question: can a slow lifeguard save your team from drowning in the current meta?

Let's address the elephant in the room: he looks ridiculous, and we love it. Gone is the serious, imposing golden armor. In its place, we have a bearded giant in a red lifeguard jacket, holding a massive wooden kickboard like it’s a tower shield. It’s campy, it’s fun, and it completely undermines the seriousness of the battlefield. The "Baywatch" vibe is strong, and frankly, if you're going to lose a raid, it might as well be to a guy who looks like he shouts "No running on the pool deck!" as a battle cry.

The costume provides a significant stat bump over the base version. With maxed stats (including LB2) pushing his defense over 1200 and health over 2200, he is undeniably sturdy. While his attack stat of roughly

(pre-LB) is still on the lower side for a 5-star, his bulk allows him to actually survive long enough to use his Slow speed skill. Compared to modern monsters with 1300+ stats across the board, he is "average" rather than "obsolete," which is a massive improvement.
The "Lifeguard" version of Sand Storm is a utility Swiss Army Knife:
215% to all enemies. Slightly better than the base, but still not a nuker.
This is a massive amount of total damage (163 per turn). While 5 turns is a long time, in a prolonged battle, this pressure adds up significantly.
This is the star of the show. A 33% boost is enormous, effectively making your Average heroes Fast and your Slow heroes manageable.
Unlike the base version, he cleanses
. This is crucial. A cleanser who also speeds up your team is a rare and valuable combination.
Slow. This is his Achilles' heel. However, once he fires, the +33% mana gen helps him (and his team) recharge much faster for the second round.
is guaranteed (unless cleansed). That's a potential 4000+ damage spread across an enemy team over 5 turns.
With the +33% buff active, your team becomes speed demons. An Average speed hero (10 tiles) drops to ~7.5 tiles, essentially becoming Fast.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
He retains the Sand Empire family bonus but swaps his class to
The 10%/20% defense bonus and healing efficiency boost make him even tankier. If paired with another Sand hero (like Roc or Rana), he becomes incredibly hard to kill with tile damage.
Like the base version, he lacks modern "start with mana" passives. However, the costume bonus itself provides stat boosts and a small mana gen bump (5%), which helps tuning his speed.
This aether power gives him healing over time at the start of the battle. This is excellent for a tank, helping him survive the initial tile dump before his healers are ready.
When released, C-Yunan was seen as a massive upgrade. The shift from "selfish tank" to "support tank" made him viable in wars again.
While his stats have held up better than the base version, "Slow" speed is a tough sell in 2026. Heroes like
He is still a top-tier pick for Rush Attack wars and tournaments. Outside of that, he is too slow for standard defense but can be a fun offensive tool if you can protect him until he fires.

Base Yunan offers healing reduction (-50%). Costume Yunan offers Cleanse + Mana Gen. In almost every scenario, enabling your own team (Cleanse/Mana) is more valuable than debuffing the enemy's healing (which can be cleansed).
The permanent stat boost from the costume is essential even if you wanted to use the base version (which you shouldn't).
As a Paladin, he gets access to defense bonuses.
Maximizes his Paladin class talent (Defense buff on damage) and keeps him alive to fire his crucial support skill.
Increases the Sand DoT significantly. Since DoT is his main damage source, this makes him more threatening.
path is safer. His job is to fire the Mana Buff and Cleanse. The damage is a bonus.
Cleanse + Mana Speed + DoT is a fantastic combination of effects in one skill.
In Very Fast modes, his +33% mana gen makes his team nearly unstoppable once the engine starts.
With Limit Breaks and the costume bonus, his stats can still compete with modern heroes.
Slow mana is his biggest liability. He can be killed or mana-controlled before he ever moves.
5 turns is a long time. It gives the enemy plenty of chances to cleanse the Sand damage.
He doesn't do much until he fires. He has no "on damage received" passives to punish enemies for hitting him.
He is S-Tier here. The mana speed buff makes your Very Fast team loop their specials endlessly.
The mana gen buff is incredible for long boss fights, helping your healers and snipers fire more often.
Good for war depth when you need a cleanser who isn't a healer.
Costumed Yunan is an "Engine" hero. Once he fires, your team shifts into high gear.
Alberich - Yunan - Heimdall. A revive/overheal/mana boost nightmare that just won't die.
Yunan + Xnolphod + Ludwig. If you get this trio going, you will never run out of mana.
His Mana Gen buff is dispellable. A simple Sabina or Caedmon ruins his setup.
Heroes like Garnet or Vanda prevent his Sand DoT from landing.
He is helpless against Hel, Proteus, or Silence effects.
Costumed Yunan is a great pull. Even if he is slow, a 5-star cleanser is invaluable for a growing roster. Level him up and use him as your tank or flank.
He is a situational tool. Don't use him on your main defense team (unless it's Rush week). However, for Rush Wars, he is worth keeping and even Limit Breaking if you lack premier Rush tanks.
"Facepalm. Guess they did not learn it from useless Azlar... 5 turns is more a nerf than a buff."
- A community reaction to the extended duration of the DoT.
"For me, the 'highlight' of the buff is the increased number of turns for the mana gen (from 3 to 5 turns)."
- A more optimistic take on the support aspect.
"I barely used him anyway... I have Arco, and he is anchored at tank."

Costumed Yunan proves that you can teach an old dog (or sand king) new tricks. While he isn't the terror of the arena he once was, his transition to a support role has aged gracefully. He provides a unique mix of cleansing and acceleration that few Nature heroes can match. He might be wearing a goofy lifeguard outfit, but in Rush Attack, he’s the one deciding who sinks and who swims.
A solid, situational hero who excels in Rush modes and PvE, but struggles to keep pace in standard competitive play.
(Note: Base version included for reference, but stick to the lifeguard.)
A comprehensive 2026 review of Costumed Yunan, the Lifeguard from the Sand Empire. We analyze his updated stats, team-wide cleanse, and massive mana generation buff.
